content.problemStatement:
You have a robot which is supposed to follow the instructions you have laid out for it. You have an array AAA of NNN **positive integers**.


The robot starts at (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) facing the positive XXX axis.
Then, for each iii from 111 to NNN:

The robot moves AiA_iAi​ steps in the direction it is facing.
After that, the robot is supposed to turn 909090 degrees clockwise.


Unfortunately, the robot was not coded correctly, and hence it cannot correctly follow the instructions correctly. Specifically, it messes up the directions to turn. After each turn, independently, it can either turn 909090 degrees clockwise or 909090 degrees counter-clockwise.

We define f(A)f(A)f(A) as the number of possible positions where the robot can end up. For example, f([1])=1f([1]) = 1f([1])=1 because the robot can only end at (1,0)(1, 0)(1,0), while f([3,2])=2f([3, 2]) = 2f([3,2])=2 because the robot can end up in either (3,2)(3, 2)(3,2) or (3,−2)(3, -2)(3,−2).

You are given an integer NNN. Does there exist an array AAA such that f(A)=N?f(A) = N?f(A)=N? Find the minimum size of an array AAA such that f(A)=Nf(A) = Nf(A)=N or print −1-1−1 if there exists none.

Note that AAA can only contain **positive integers**.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum size of an array AAA such that f(A)=Nf(A) = Nf(A)=N, or −1-1−1 if no array exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The array A=[3,2]A = [3, 2]A=[3,2] satisfies the conditions as mentioned earlier.
Test Case 2 : There is no array AAA with f(A)=3f(A) = 3f(A)=3.
